rationale: The citing paper centers on referral-program behavior and mechanism testing (social appropriateness, norm salience, and referral-stage outcomes), while the cited paper develops and tests a mechanism model for referral programs (reputational benefits and action costs across referral/uptake stages). This is a direct conceptual overlap in core constructs and likely serves as prior mechanism/theory evidence rather than a generic mention. The reference is also an exact DOI match (10.1177/0022243719888440).
